annotation.message: An operand identifier prescription is hiding another identifier of the same name.
An operand identifier prescription is hiding another identifier of the same name.

def.name: Patient
Patient
Palliative Care Lookback Period

a:s.a:s:
/* ** Routine #1 ** CDC 2022 General Inclusion Criteria Routine ** ** Definition | Answer to Proceed | Details | Data (Terminology) Requirement ** ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ** Age < 18 years? | No | Calculate age from date of birth; exclude | Date of birth ** | | patients with age less than 18 years at the | ** | | time of the prescription | ** ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ** Evidence of | No | Look for documented findings consistent | Sickle cell disease conditions ** sickle cell disease? | | with those listed in the sickle cell | ** | | value set | ** ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ** Active cancer | No | See sub-routine 3 | See sub-routine 3 ** treatment? | | | ** ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ** Conditions Likely | No | Look for patients with active conditions in | Conditions likely terminal for opioid ** Terminal for opioid | | the value set end-of-life-conditions | prescribing ** prescribing present? | | | ** ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ** */
define "Palliative Care Lookback Period":

def.name: Patient Age Less Than 18

condition.name: Age Less than 18 Years Is Enabled

def.name: Condition Positive for Sickle Cell
Encounter Period
Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period
Number of Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period

a:s.a:s: define "Number of Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period":
"Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period"

def.name: Has Two or More Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period

"Number of Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period"

operand.name: Number of Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period

def.name: Has Active Cancer Diagnosis on Problem List

a:s.a:s: define "Has Active Cancer Diagnosis on Problem List":

def.name: Is Active Cancer Treatment?

a:s.a:s: "Active Cancer Treatment Encounters Condition Is Enabled"
"Has Two or More Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period"
"Has Active Cancer Diagnosis on Problem List"

condition.name: Active Cancer Treatment Encounters Condition Is Enabled

operand.name: Has Two or More Encounters with Cancer Diagnosis During Encounter Period
Has Active Cancer Diagnosis on Problem List

def.name: Conditions Likely Terminal for Opioid Prescribing

def.name: Prescription/Dispense Inclusion Period
Report Inclusion Period
Opioid RX with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days

"Opioid RX with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days"

def.name: Opioid Dispense with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days

a:s.a:s: Common."Opioid analgesics with ambulatory misuse potential"

valueset.name: Opioid analgesics with ambulatory misuse potential

def.name: Has Opioid Dispense with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days

"Opioid Dispense with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days"

def.name: Is Opioid Naive?

a:s.a:s: // NOTE: MedicationStatement-dependent logic comment out due to bug in Publisher not supporting MedicationStatement in AllTypes. define "Is Opioid Naive?":
"Has Opioid RX with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days"
/* and not ("Has Report of Opioid with Ambulatory Care Abuse Potential Reported in Past 90 Days") */ and
"Has Opioid Dispense with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days"

operand.name: Has Opioid RX with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days
Has Opioid Dispense with Ambulatory Abuse Potential In Past 90 Days

def.name: Is Acute Pain Prescription?
Acute Pain Prescription Greater Than 7 Days?

a:s.a:s: // should allow DateTime precision, but not working in the Ruler specifically define function "Acute Pain Prescription Greater Than 7 Days?"(prescription FHIR.MedicationRequest):
